artifact_purpose: Defines the canonical human-facing spreadsheet interface for the comment resolution matrix, including exact headers, order, and adjudication semantics.

headers.description: Sequential identifier from the agency submission; used to map dispositions back to the original row without renumbering.
headers.notes: Preserve agency-provided numbering to avoid misalignment during adjudication.

headers.description: Initials of the reviewer submitting the comment; keeps attribution while keeping sheets concise.
Spreadsheet revision anchor linking this comment to the working paper revision under review (e.g., rev1, rev2).
headers.notes: Required to map adjudication back to the correct working paper revision; blank cells in single-revision runs map to rev1.

headers.description: Section number or title reference from the working paper.
Page number from the working paper referenced by the comment.
Line number or range within the referenced page.
Classification of the comment for routing and prioritization.
Context, rationale, or references supplied by the agency.
Verbatim text the agency proposes to replace or add.
Adjudication output capturing NTIA reviewer notes or rationale.
Adjudication output describing the resolution decision (e.g., Accepted, Partially Accepted, Out of Scope).
Final resolution text or action to be taken for the comment.

metadata_handling.visible_metadata_policy: Do not add visible metadata columns (e.g., provenance ids, validation flags) to the primary sheet unless formally added to the contract.
metadata_handling.sidecar_policy: Store machine metadata such as provenance ids, validation states, run ids, and review states in sidecar JSON/YAML files that travel with the spreadsheet when needed.
metadata_handling.hidden_sheet_policy: If spreadsheet tools require embedded metadata, place it in a hidden worksheet; never interleave metadata columns into the canonical header row.

compatibility.notes: Downstream repos must import/export with these exact headers and this exact order; no silent renames in user-facing artifacts.
compatibility.export_guidance: Internal models may use normalized keys but exports must preserve the official headers and ordering.

validation_rules.revision_mapping_rule: Report Version is the revision anchor tying each row to the working paper revision under adjudication; blank inputs in single-revision runs map to rev1.
